title: codivalia patula
intro: Codivalia patula, also known as the Codia tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Codivalia patula include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin infections, among others. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
